# Fix the readiness probe to use HTTPS with -k
d['spec']['template']['spec']['containers'][0]['readinessProbe']['exec']['command'] = [
    '/bin/sh',
    '-c',
    'curl -s -k --max-time 5 -u elastic:\${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate | grep -q \'\"username\":\"elastic\"\'',
]

# Remove status and other server-managed fields
d.pop('status', None)

with open('/tmp/auth-checker-patched.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(d, f)

print('done')
print(json.dumps(d['spec']['template']['spec']['containers'][0]['readinessProbe'], indent=2))
"
kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f /tmp/auth-checker-patched.json 2>/dev/null

content.content: deployment.apps/auth-checker configured

tool_use_result.stdout: deployment.apps/auth-checker configured

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  READY=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get deployment auth-checker -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  PODS=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=auth-checker --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1" "$2}' | tr '\n' ' | ')
  echo "Attempt $i: readyReplicas=$READY pods=[$PODS]"
  if [ "$READY" = "1" ]; then
    echo "auth-checker is Ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
